title: Small Business Owner: Succession and Retirement Gap
goals: Build adequate retirement savings to retire at 55
Protect business and personal assets with proper insurance
constraints: 50% of net worth in illiquid business equity
No qualified retirement plan for 12-year-old business
key_risks: No buy-sell agreement — partner's death creates unworkable partnership with heirs
Key-man insurance lapsed — business credit line at risk
60% of net worth in illiquid single business
No disability insurance — income fully exposed

planning_issues.description: $85K in IRA after 12 years of $230K income; Solo 401k can shelter $50,000+/year but was never established
No buy-sell agreement; key-man insurance expired 2 years ago; no owner disability insurance
$310,000 business equity (50% stake) is 60% of net worth — illiquid, undiversified, single industry
No succession plan — $620,000 business value at risk without transition planning

key_recommendations: Draft buy-sell agreement within 90 days — cross-purchase, life insurance funded
Reinstate key-man life insurance on both partners ($300-500K each)
Add disability buyout insurance for both partners
Engage M&A advisor for 5-year succession planning (target retirement at 55)
follow_up_questions: Business succession preference: third-party sale, family succession, or partner buyout?
Has the business been formally valued by a certified business appraiser?
Does James (partner) have a conflicting succession timeline?
Is key-man insurance a covenant requirement on the business credit line?
outcome_summary: Solo 401k established with $47,000 contributed year one. Buy-sell agreement signed and funded with $500K life policies on each partner. Key-man insurance reinstated.
key_lesson: Small business owners systematically underfund personal retirement while over-concentrating in illiquid business equity. Solo 401k plus buy-sell agreement with life insurance is the minimum viable risk mitigation framework.
